text: Sleep is a fundamental component of performance optimization and should be treated as an important part of training and recovery programs.
Sleep plays a crucial role in both physical and cognitive performance in athletes.
Adequate sleep is an important factor in reducing risk of injury in athletes.
Poor sleep is prevalent among elite athletes, suggesting systematic screening and sleep hygiene interventions should be part of athlete support programs.
Sport culture often undervalues healthy sleep, and organizations should actively promote sleep as a performance and recovery tool.
